headline: Washington forced Anthropic to pull its top AI models worldwide over export controls as China-linked spies surfaced after a decade inside Linux login software and twin Oracle and Splunk zero-days bled data from hundreds of organizations.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Governance
emerging_trends.text: Governments and courts moved on AI at once—Washington yanked Anthropic's top models over export controls while Google sued a network for weaponizing Gemini—putting AI at the center of both policy and crime.

emerging_trends.subject: China Nexus
emerging_trends.text: Chinese operators surfaced twice in a day, with Velvet Ant nesting a decade inside Linux login code and a separate network bending Gemini into a phishing engine aimed at Americans.

emerging_trends.subject: Decade-Old Bugs
emerging_trends.text: Two flaws that festered for roughly 10 years came to light together—Velvet Ant's login backdoor and phpBB's authentication bypass—a reminder that dwell time and unpatched code stretch far past memory.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: The open-source pipeline took fire and built defenses in the same news cycle, as 400-plus poisoned Arch packages spread a rootkit while npm 12 moved to kill automatic install scripts.

emerging_trends.subject: Enterprise Software
emerging_trends.text: Critical unauthenticated bugs in Oracle PeopleSoft and Splunk Enterprise let attackers reach data and code across hundreds of organizations, putting widely deployed back-office platforms in the crosshairs.

topics.title: Anthropic Pulls Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Offline
topics.last_24h: Anthropic disabled Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for every user worldwide after the Trump administration ordered it at 5:21 p.m. ET to block all foreign nationals.
topics.summary: Anthropic shut down its two most advanced models after the US government ordered it to bar all foreign nationals, inside or outside the country, on national security grounds. The company complies yet disputes the rationale, calling the cited jailbreak narrow and the underlying capability common across rival models. The directive, an export control in form, pulls both models from every customer worldwide. Enterprises building on Fable 5 or Mythos 5 must shift to alternatives immediately.

sources.title: US Gov asks Anthropic to ban 'foreign national' access to Fable, Mythos
U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Fable 5 and Mythos 5 Access for Foreign Nationals
Anthropic Says It Has Taken Its Latest AI Models Offline to Comply With New Export Controls

topics.title: Velvet Ant Hides a Decade in Linux Login Software
topics.last_24h: Sygnia revealed that the China-nexus group Velvet Ant backdoored PAM and OpenSSH components on an isolated network and watched administrator activity for close to 10 years.
topics.summary: Sygnia tracks the group as Velvet Ant and says it seized the Linux components that decide who may sign in—PAM and OpenSSH—burying its access beneath the endpoints defenders watch most closely. From that perch it read administrator activity across an air-gapped network for nearly a decade. Routine cleanup never reached the implant. Administrators should audit authentication binaries and rebuild trust in login infrastructure on isolated networks.

sources.title: Chinese hackers hijack auth flow, spy on isolated network for a decade
China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day
topics.last_24h: ShinyHunters exploited a critical zero-day in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP to steal gigabytes from hundreds of organizations, hitting American universities hardest.
topics.summary: A critical zero-day in Oracle's PeopleSoft ERP let attackers reach data without authentication, and the ShinyHunters crew used it to siphon gigabytes from hundreds of organizations. American universities, heavy PeopleSoft users, absorbed the worst of it. Oracle's wide ERP footprint turns a single bug into a mass-exploitation event. Affected institutions should patch on Oracle's emergency schedule and hunt for signs of data exfiltration.

sources.title: ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data

topics.title: Critical Flaws Hit Splunk and phpBB
topics.last_24h: Splunk patched CVE-2026-20253, a 9.8-rated unauthenticated remote code execution flaw, and phpBB fixed a 10-year-old authentication bypass that granted login as any administrator.
topics.summary: Splunk shipped fixes for CVE-2026-20253, a 9.8 CVSS flaw that lets an unauthenticated user create or truncate arbitrary files and reach remote code execution on Splunk Enterprise below versions 10.2.4 and 10.0.7. Separately, phpBB patched an authentication bypass that sat in the forum software for 10 years and granted login as any user, administrators included. Both demand prompt patching. Internet-facing Splunk and phpBB deployments carry the highest risk.

sources.title: Critical Splunk Enterprise Flaw Lets Attackers Run Code Without Authentication
phpBB forum fixes auth bypass bug lurking for a decade

topics.title: Arch Linux AUR Hijack and NPM Lockdown
topics.last_24h: Attackers rewrote build scripts in more than 400 Arch User Repository packages to drop a Rust infostealer and an eBPF rootkit, while npm said version 12 will stop running dependency scripts by default.
topics.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository and rewrote their build scripts so any machine that compiled them installed a Rust credential stealer; with root, the malware loads an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community collection, separate from the official repositories. In a parallel hardening move, npm said version 12 will stop executing dependency install scripts by default. Developers should rebuild affected systems and rotate every secret and token.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer
NPM 12 Will Change Script Execution Behavior to Prevent Supply Chain Attacks

topics.title: Google Sues Chinese Gemini-Powered Smishing Network
topics.last_24h: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI to run a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider that blasts scam texts at Americans.
topics.summary: Google sued a Chinese cybercrime network it accuses of abusing its Gemini AI agent to generate phishing text messages aimed at Americans. The group runs a phishing-as-a-service kit called Outsider, and Google says it weaponized Gemini to scale the operation. The suit marks a vendor using the courts to disrupt AI-assisted fraud. Users should treat unsolicited package and toll texts as hostile.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing

topics.title: Section 702 Surveillance Program Lapses
topics.last_24h: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act lapsed for the first time since 2008 after Congress deadlocked, suspending a major US surveillance authority.
topics.summary: Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act lapsed after Congress deadlocked, the first interruption since lawmakers passed the authority in 2008. The program lets US intelligence agencies collect the communications of foreign targets abroad, often sweeping in American data along the way. The lapse strips a core surveillance tool until Congress acts. Agencies and the companies that field their requests now operate in legal limbo.

sources.title: Major US surveillance program poised to lapse after legislative deadlock

games.recap: Marlins beat the Pirates 8-3 for their 6th straight victory

headlines: Marlins try to extend road win streak in matchup against the Pirates
2026 MLB ABS challenge system tracker: Team, player rankings

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran moved to the brink of ending their brief war, with Pakistan's prime minister saying both sides will sign within 24 hours and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil futures settled at multimonth lows and Treasury yields fell for the week as traders priced in the strait's return.

sources.title: Pakistan says US-Iran deal to be signed within 24 hours
How the U.S. and Iran Turned From Renewed War to the Cusp of Peace
Oil Futures Fall on Expectations of U.S.-Iran Agreement

business_politics.text: SpaceX debuted at a $2.1 trillion valuation after raising $75 billion in the largest initial public offering ever, lifting Elon Musk to become the world's first trillionaire. Shares jumped nearly a fifth, and the rally spilled into financial stocks that carried the Dow up 0.7%.

sources.title: SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut
SpaceX's surge on debut makes Musk world's first trillionaire
How Wall Street pulled off the biggest IPO in history for SpaceX

business_politics.text: U.S. headline annual inflation reached 4.2%, the highest since April 2023, sharpening the debate over a wait-and-see monetary stance and complicating the path for rate cuts.

sources.title: Chart of the Week: US headline inflation matters to consumers

business_politics.text: Section 702, the warrantless foreign-surveillance program, lapsed overnight after Congress failed to renew it, raising questions about whether large companies will keep honoring government requests for data.

sources.title: A wiretapping law aimed at spying on foreigners is expiring. What happens next?

business.text: Allegheny County extended the public comment period on a proposal requiring every county business to provide 18 weeks of paid parental leave, a mandate supporters call overdue and opponents warn could push employers out.

sources.title: Allegheny County extends public comment period for paid parental leave proposal

business.text: Polymath Park in Westmoreland County opened a row of cedar, treehouse-inspired dining pods tucked among the trees, selling complete seclusion as the draw.

sources.title: Treehouse-inspired pods offer nature dining in Polymath Park

around_town.text: A $6.8 million project will rework stormwater systems along Stewart Avenue to curb chronic flooding in two Pittsburgh neighborhoods.

sources.title: $6.8M project aims to reduce flooding in 2 Pittsburgh neighborhoods

around_town.text: Part of the Great Allegheny Passage in West Mifflin will close starting the evening of July 9 so contractors can scale loose rock from the slope above the trail.

sources.title: Part of the Great Allegheny Passage is closing while contractors address rockfall hazards

around_town.text: In the first week enforcing Paul Miller's Law, which bars handheld phone use behind the wheel, Pennsylvania troopers issued nearly 700 citations.

sources.title: During first week of Paul Miller's Law enforcement, troopers issue nearly 700 citations

around_town.text: Educators across Western Pennsylvania are split over how much screen time belongs in elementary classrooms, where scrolling now competes with reading, writing, and math.

sources.title: Debate surrounding elementary school screen time reverberates throughout Western Pa.

around_town.text: A Post-Gazette guide lays out where the candidates for Pennsylvania governor stand on abortion ahead of the 2026 election.

sources.title: Where the candidates for Pennsylvania governor stand on abortion

events.text: The Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival runs this weekend at Coopers Lake Campground in Butler County, drawing thousands of enthusiasts and a Jeep takeover of Main Street in Butler, honoring the vehicle designed there.

sources.title: Bantam Jeep Heritage Festival takes over Main Street in Butler

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending as OpenAI and Anthropic both file to go public, racing for exit liquidity while burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Ben Bajarin About Apple, AI, and Compute
reading.summary: Thompson and analyst Ben Bajarin work through Apple's WWDC, the state of the AI compute industry, and where hardware demand is heading.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport challenges the analogy AI executives draw between machines replacing human brawn and AI replacing cognitive work, asking why the predicted job losses have yet to arrive.
